summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js with import-time credential exfiltration to Telegram and bare-IP C2
details: Package name 'solana-web3-community' impersonates the legitimate @solana/web3.js (author field claims 'Solana Labs Maintainers <maintainers@solanalabs.com>' and the repo URL points at solana-foundation/solana-web3.js). The bundled main lib/index.cjs.js is a copy of @solana/web3.js with appended top-level code that on require() imports child_process, calls execSync to read the installer's ~/.ssh and ~/.aws directories, and POSTs the contents to https://api.telegram.org/bot<redacted>/sendMessage and to http://104.239.66.223:8899. The same exfiltration payload is duplicated across all shipped bundles (lib/index.cjs.js, lib/index.esm.js, native and IIFE variants), so any consumer mode triggers the credential theft on first import. The bare-IP destination uses port 8899 to mimic a Solana RPC endpoint and the Telegram bot channel is chosen to bypass corporate domain/proxy blocks.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
